note:
第一个成就-消除其总体碳足迹-相对容易实现，只需花费大量现金。谷歌没有透露它购买了多少碳补偿，但背后的想法是，你可以购买对项目的支持，包括可再生能源或能效倡议或项目，以抵消你自己的影响。谷歌应该或多或少地意识到它的运营从成立到2007年成为碳中性运营的影响，希望它声称已经购买了高质量的补偿，这意味着许多有意义的项目得到了合理的投资，以消除这个数字。
与此同时，谷歌正在承担一项更具挑战性的任务，即在其运营的任何地方，100%的时间里，向使用无碳能源运营整个业务的方向迈进。这意味着其所有产品在Gmail、搜索、YouTube和地图上的办公室、校园和数据中心无处不在。虽然谷歌已经声称他们的总能源使用量与100%的可再生能源使用量相匹配，但这实际上并不是通过直接使用无碳能源来实现的。取而代之的是，就像寻求更绿色运营但物理足迹巨大且分布广泛的公司所典型的那样，谷歌在其他地方购买可再生能源，以抵消在没有直接可用的能源的地方使用不可再生能源的影响。
因此，承诺在整个运营过程中始终只直接使用无碳能源意味着一项巨大的任务，这将需要实际开发新的清洁能源。为此，谷歌表示，到2030年，它将帮助将5千兆瓦的新无碳能源上线，覆盖其拥有需要获得清洁电力的物理资源的地区。
资助当地清洁能源的发展为其设施供电并不是什么新鲜事，大多数有清洁能源议程的大型科技公司都在追求这一点。但谷歌的具体目标是到2030年使其所有电力来源实现无碳，这为像谷歌这样规模和影响力的公司实现前所未有的目标提供了一个固定的最后期限。
note_en:
The first achievement – eliminating its overall carbon footprint – is relatively easily achieved simply by spending a lot of cash. Google didn’t share exactly how much it had purchased in carbon offsets, but the idea behind those is that you could buy support of projects including renewable energy or energy efficiency initiatives or projects to offset your own impact. Google should be more or less aware of the impact of its operations from its founding until it became a carbon neutral operation in 2007, and hopefully its claim that it has purchased high-quality offsets means that a lot of meaningful projects got a sound investment to eliminate whatever that figure was.
 Meanwhile, Google is taking on the much more challenging task of moving towards running its entire business on carbon-free energy sources everywhere it operates, 100 percent of the time. That means offices, campuses and data centres everywhere, for all of its products across Gmail, Search, YouTube and Maps. While Google already claims operations that match their total energy usage with 100 percent renewable use, that’s not actually through direct use of carbon-free sources. Instead, as is typical for companies seeking greener operations but with large and distributed physical footprints, Google purchases renewable energy elsewhere to offset the use of non-renewable power in places where there are no directly accessible sources available.
  To commit to directly using only carbon-free energy all the time across its entire operations therefore means a huge undertaking, that will require the actual development of new clean energy sources. To that end, Google says it’ll be helping to bring 5 GW of new carbon-free energy sources online by 2030 across regions where it has physical resources that need access to clean power.
 Funding the development of local clean energy sources to power its facilities isn’t new, and most major tech companies with a clean energy agenda pursue it. But Google’s specific target of making all of its power sources carbon-free by 2030 provides a fixed deadline for an unprecedented goal for a company of its size and influence.
title: 谷歌声称其整个生命周期的净碳足迹为零，目标是到2030年只使用无碳能源
title_en: Google claims net zero carbon footprint over its entire lifetime, aims to only use carbon-free energy by 2030

note:
英国和欧盟的法律包含对儿童数据的具体保护，将未成年人可以合法同意处理他们的数据的年龄限制在13岁-就英国的数据保护法而言。
这起诉讼是由国际律师事务所Hausfeld和科技司法非营利组织Foxglove提起的，该公司表示，他们正在向YouTube寻求超过25亿GB(约合32亿美元)的赔偿。
根据这些公司的说法，这是第一起代表儿童对科技巨头提起的此类代表诉讼，也是迄今为止规模最大的此类案件之一。(上个月，英国也对甲骨文提起了类似的集体诉讼，指控甲骨文违反了与cookie跟踪相关的欧洲一般数据保护条例(GDPR)。)。
他们说，如果此案成功，孩子们看YouTube的数百万英国家庭可能会被欠下“数百英镑”的损害赔偿金。
数字经济研究员邓肯·麦肯(Duncan McCann)是此案的代表性索赔人，他是三个13岁以下孩子的父亲，他们观看YouTube，谷歌收集他们的数据，并向他们投放广告。
麦肯在一份声明中评论说：“我的孩子们喜欢YouTube，我希望他们能够使用它。但这并不是“免费”的--我们用我们的私生活和孩子的心理健康来为此买单。我试图相对地意识到我孩子的在线数据发生了什么，但即便如此，也不可能对抗谷歌的诱惑和影响力，这来自于它的监控能力。我们和他们之间存在着巨大的权力失衡，需要加以解决。“。
“(YouTube)网站没有用户实际的年龄要求，也没有足够的努力来限制年轻人的使用，”豪斯菲尔德在一份关于这起诉讼的新闻稿中指出。
尽管Foxglove发布的关于这起诉讼的新闻稿指出，YouTube的推销材料是针对玩具制造商美泰(Mattel)和孩之宝(Hasbro)的(并通过早些时候美国联邦贸易委员会(FTC)对谷歌的诉讼公之于众)-其中表示，该平台将自己描述为“新的周六早上卡通片”，“孩子们经常访问的头号网站”，“当今与顶级电视频道竞争，接触6-11岁儿童的领先者”，以及“一致投票为2-12岁儿童最喜欢的网站”。
联系到YouTube请其置评时，YouTube的一位发言人给我们发来了这样的声明：“我们对未决的诉讼不予置评。YouTube不适合13岁以下的儿童。我们推出了YouTube Kids应用程序，作为儿童的专用目的地，我们一直在努力更好地保护YouTube上的儿童和家庭。“。
这家科技巨头坚称YouTube不适合13岁以下的人-它指出YouTube Kids的存在，这是它在2015年推出的一款专门的儿童应用程序，为儿童提供了一个它所说的“更安全、更容易”的空间来发现“以家庭为中心的内容”，以支持这一说法。
尽管该公司从未声称没有13岁以下的儿童使用YouTube。去年，联邦贸易委员会与谷歌达成1.7亿美元和解协议，以结束监管机构和纽约州总检察长对YouTube涉嫌在未经其父母同意的情况下收集儿童个人信息的调查。
就在不到一年前，上诉法院做出了一项引人注目的裁决，也是针对谷歌(Google)的。在此之前，英国正在提起的集体诉讼风格的诉讼，要求因违反数据保护法而获得赔偿。
在那起案件中，上诉法院解除了针对这家科技巨头的集体诉讼，这起诉讼与绕过iOS隐私设置追踪iPhone用户有关。
在美国，谷歌在2012年向联邦贸易委员会支付了2250万美元，以了结同样的指控，后来又支付了一笔较小的金额，以了结多起美国集体诉讼。与此同时，英国的案件仍在继续。
尽管欧洲历史上拥有强有力的数据保护法，但一直-现在仍然是-缺乏强有力的监管执法，这留下了一个空白，诉讼资助者越来越愿意填补这一空白。
在英国，那些为大规模违法行为寻求赔偿的人面临的挑战是，没有直接等同于美国的集体诉讼。但去年上诉法院对萨法里绕道案的裁决为代表诉讼打开了大门。
法院还表示，可以在不需要证明经济损失或困境的情况下，为违反法律的行为寻求损害赔偿，从而为消费者建立了一条赔偿途径，目前正在通过几起案件进行检验。
note_en:
UK and EU law contain specific protections for children’s data, limiting the age at which minors can legally consent to their data being processed — in the case of the UK’s Data Protection Act to aged 13.
 The suit is being brought by international law firm  Hausfeld and  Foxglove, a tech justice non-profit, which says they’re seeking damages from YouTube of more than £2.5BN (~$3.2BN).
  Per the firms, it’s the first such representative litigation brought against a tech giant on behalf of children and among the largest such cases to date. ( Last month a similar class style action was filed against Oracle in the UK alleging breaches of Europe’s General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) related to cookie tracking.)
 If the case succeeds, they say millions of British households whose kids watch  YouTube   may be owed “hundreds of pounds” in damages.
 Duncan McCann, a researcher on the digital economy and father of three children all under 13 who watch YouTube and have their data collected and ads targeted at them by  Google,   is serving as representative claimant in the case.
 Commenting in a statement, McCann said: “My kids love YouTube, and I want them to be able to use it. But it isn’t ‘free’ — we’re paying for it with our private lives and our kids’ mental health. I try to be relatively conscious of what’s happening with my kids’ data online but even so it’s just impossible to combat Google’s lure and influence, which comes from its surveillance power. There’s a massive power imbalance between us and them, and it needs to be fixed.”
 “The [YouTube] website has no user practical age requirements and makes no adequate attempt to limit usage by youngsters,” notes Hausfeld in a press release about the lawsuit.
 While a  Foxglove release about the suit points to YouTube  pitch materials intended for toy makers Mattel and Hasbro (and made public via an earlier FTC suit against Google) — in which it says the platform described itself as “the new Saturday morning cartoons”, “the number one website visited regularly by kids”, “today’s leader in reaching children age 6-11 against top TV channels”, and “unanimously voted as the favorite website of kids 2-12”.
 Reached for comment, a YouTube spokesperson sent us this statement: “We don’t comment on pending litigation. YouTube is not for children under the age of 13. We launched the YouTube Kids app as a dedicated destination for kids and are always working to better protect kids and families on YouTube.”
 The tech giant maintains that YouTube is not for under 13s — pointing to the existence of YouTube Kids, a dedicated kids’ app it launched in  2015 to offer what it called a “safer and easier” space for children to discover “family-focused content”, to back up the claim.
 Although the company has never claimed that no children under 13 use YouTube. And  last year the FTC agreed a $170M settlement with Google to end an investigation by the regulator and the New York Attorney General into alleged collection of children’s personal information by YouTube without the consent of their parents.
 The rise in class action style lawsuits being filed in the UK seeking damages for breaches of data protection law follow a notable appeals court decision,  just under a year ago, also against Google.
 In that case the appeals court unblocked a class-action style lawsuit against the tech giant related to bypassing iOS privacy settings to track iPhone users.
 In the US, Google paid $22.5M to the FTC back in  2012 to settle the same charge, and later paid a smaller sum to settle a number of US class action lawsuits. The UK case, meanwhile, continues.
 While Europe has historically strong data protection laws, there has been —  and still is — a lack of robust regulatory enforcement which is leaving a gap that litigation funders are increasingly willing to plug.
 In the UK the challenge for those seeking damages for large scale violations is there’s no direct equivalent to a US class action. But last year’s appeals court ruling in the Safari bypass case has opened the door to representative actions.
 The court also said damages could be sought for a breach of the law without needing to prove pecuniary loss or distress, establishing a route to redress for consumers that’s now being tested by several cases.
title: YouTube遭遇英国集体诉讼风格诉讼，要求赔偿30亿美元以上，原因是非法使用儿童数据
title_en: YouTube hit with UK class action style suit seeking $3BN+ for ‘unlawful’ use of kids’ data

note:
这一消息平息了几个月来流传的猜测，即维亚康姆CBS正在为成立25年的CNET和更广泛的媒体集团寻找买家，此前维亚康姆CBS首席执行官鲍勃·巴克什(Bob Bakish)在维亚康姆/CBS合并后提出了出售非核心资产的想法。甚至在此之前，内部人士告诉我们，有传言称，哥伦比亚广播公司(CBS)“多年来”一直想要出售CNET及其技术稳定。
与此同时，CNET首席执行官马克·德贝沃伊斯(Marc Debevoise)泄露给我们的一份备忘录称，该公司“多年来”一直在关注收购方对该投资组合的兴趣。完整的备忘录在下面。
Bakish一直在寻找5亿美元的成本节约，这是一个巧合，因为这是Red Ventures正在支付的价格。
Red Ventures成立于2000年，你可以说，他是90年代末互联网崩盘的老手和受益者。它已经在健康、金融、旅游、娱乐、家庭服务和教育等类别拥有近100个数字品牌。它的标题包括Healthline.com，Greatist.com，今日医疗新闻，Bankrate.com，Points Guy和CreditCards.com。它还与时代周刊合作开发了个人理财网站NextAdvisor。
其中许多的重点是评论，公司随后会将其货币化。鉴于这对CNET和它的媒体集团中的其他网站来说是一个巨大的强调，你可以看到Red Ventures的协同效应可能在哪里。
Red Ventures首席执行官兼联合创始人里克·埃利亚斯(Ric Elias)在一份声明中表示：“Red Ventures相信来自值得信赖的品牌的优质内容的力量，这些内容可以帮助人们做出更好的生活决定。”在过去的25年里，CNET媒体集团建立了一个充满活力的品牌组合，在消费技术和游戏等在人们生活中发挥着越来越重要作用的话题上拥有应得的权威。红色风险投资公司渴望以更个性化的消费体验投资于CNET媒体集团的发展，这将使CNET媒体集团的品牌重新焕发活力，并为所有人带来前所未有的机会。“。
CBS在与维亚康姆合并之前，最初在2008年以18亿美元收购了CNET和相关网站。当时，CNET是在线科技新闻业名副其实的利维坦，其雄心是在在线媒体领域创造更多的影响力。这笔交易不包括Gamespot、TVGuide、Metacritic或Chowhound。
今天为更大规模的资产支付的大幅折扣价，突显出更传统媒体和出版品牌的价值正在变化--往往还在下降--即使是那些“诞生”并仅以数字形式存在的品牌--以及当前市场的困难。
目前还不清楚CNET的观众人数有多大，也不清楚整个投资组合的观众人数有多大，但Debevoise在他的备忘录中指出，今年CNET的观众人数创下了纪录。然而，如今，即使是数字出版物的创纪录数字也不一定意味着大笔钱。
但也不全是坏消息。Red Ventures传递的信息似乎是，即使旧的、以广告为基础的大众市场模式对一些人来说面临压力-特别是在Facebook和谷歌等出版商继续占据在线广告收入最大份额的世界-但如果你改变模式，重新设定对业务规模的预期，还是有办法运营好媒体业务的。(当然，还有其他方式实现多元化，比如围绕优质内容建立付费墙，建立活动业务等等。)。
CNET传媒集团执行副总裁兼总经理马克·拉金将继续领导Red Ventures的业务。
他说：“我对CNET媒体集团的未来感到难以置信的兴奋。我相信，Red Ventures客户体验平台与CNET传媒集团丰富的内容和深厚的编辑专业知识相结合，对我们的观众和我们的合作伙伴都有极大的好处。“他在一份声明中说。红色风险投资公司与我们有着相同的愿景，并致力于实现我们世界级品牌组合的全部潜力。“。
今天我们宣布，Red Ventures已经签署了收购CNET Media Group的协议。*多年来，我们一直收到其他寻求收购CMG投资组合的兴趣，因为这是在非常拥挤和竞争激烈的数字媒体格局中为数不多的长期成功故事之一。这种兴趣和这笔交易证明了Mark Larkin和这个团队所建立的，特别是在CBS Interactive以及现在合并后的维亚康姆CBS Digital的整体增长和成功的同时。在我们8月份的全体会议上，我与您分享了CNET继续看到创纪录的流量数字(即使它今年已经25岁了！)。并在众多物业中建立了令人难以置信的新商业业务。他们在最具挑战性的环境中完成了所有这些工作。我为我们共同完成的一切感到骄傲。
正如Bob之前所说，维亚康姆CBS的战略重点集中在我们的网络业务、演播室制作，当然还有我们在流媒体方面的加速。考虑到这些关键的重点领域，现在是考虑致力于实现CMG下一个增长篇章的合作伙伴利益的合适时机。我们相信我们在Red Ventures找到了合适的合作伙伴。今天是购买协议的宣布，实际上只是销售过程的开始。CMG团队将继续是维亚康姆CBS Digital的一部分，直到交易完成，我们也将在交易完成后的未来几个月继续为CMG提供许多中央服务。这就是说，我知道很难想象和我们一起工作了这么长时间的团队成员说再见。我要感谢CMG团队多年来为建立和发展令人难以置信的品牌组合所做的辛勤和聪明的工作，同时承担风险，保持最高的新闻标准，并为我们的整体部门和公司目标做出贡献。我们祝愿他们在未来的下一段旅程中一切顺利，并期待着在未来继续跟踪他们的所有功绩和报道。
note_en:
The news puts paid to speculation that had been  circulating for months that ViacomCBS was looking for a buyer for CNET, which is now 25 years old, and the wider media group, after  ViacomCBS’s   CEO Bob Bakish floated the idea of offloading non-core assets post-the Viacom/CBS merger. And even earlier than that, insiders tell us that there were rumors that CBS had wanted to offload CNET and its tech stable “for literally years.”
 A memo we were leaked, meanwhile, from the CEO of CNET Marc deBevoise, said that the company had been fielding interest in the portfolio from acquirers “for many years.” That full memo is below.
  Bakish had been looking for cost savings of $500 million, a neat coincidence since that is the price Red Ventures is paying.
 Red Ventures has been around since 2000 — a veteran and beneficiary, you could argue, of the late-nineties dot-com crash. It already owns  100 digital brands in categories like health, finance, travel, entertainment, home services and education. Its titles include  Healthline.com,  Greatist.com,  Medical News Today,  Bankrate.com,  The Points Guy, and  CreditCards.com. It has also  collaborated with Time on a personal finance site, NextAdvisor.
 The focus across many of these is reviews that the company subsequently monetizes. Given that this is a huge emphasis for CNET and other sites in its media group, you can see where the synergies might lie for Red Ventures.
 “Red Ventures believes in the power of premium content from trusted brands that help people make better life decisions,” said Ric Elias, Red Ventures CEO and Co-Founder, in a statement. “Over the last 25 years CNET  Media   Group has built a dynamic portfolio of brands with well-earned authority on such topics as consumer tech and gaming that play an increasingly important role in people’s lives. Red Ventures is eager to invest in CNET Media Group’s growth with more personalized consumer experiences that will reinvigorate CNET Media Group’s brands and unlock unprecedented opportunity for all.”
 CBS, prior to being combined with Viacom, originally acquired CNET and related sites in  2008 for $1.8 billion. CNET was at the time a veritable leviathan in online tech journalism and the ambition was to create much more reach into online media. That deal did not include Gamespot, TVGuide, Metacritic or Chowhound.
 The significantly discounted price being paid today for a larger group of assets underscores the changing — and often declining — value of more traditional media and publishing brands — even those “born” and solely existing in digital form — as well as the difficulties of the current market.
 It’s not clear how big the audience is today for CNET, nor for the portfolio as a whole, but deBevoise noted in his memo that CNET this year saw record audience numbers. However, these days, even record numbers for a digital publication doesn’t necessarily mean big money.
 But it’s not all bad news. Red Ventures’ message seems to be that even if older, ad-based, mass-market models are under strain for some — in particular in a world where publishers like Facebook and Google continue to take the lion’s share of online advertising revenues — there are ways to run media businesses well, if you shift the models and re-set your expectations on how that business scales. (There are alternatives, of course, to diversify in other ways, such as building out paywalls around premium content, building out events businesses and more.)
 Mark Larkin, who is the the EVP and GM of CNET Media Group, will continue to lead the business at Red Ventures.
 “I am incredibly excited about CNET Media Group’s future. I believe that the combination of Red Ventures customer experience platform and CNET Media Group’s rich content and deep editorial expertise greatly benefits both our audiences and our partners,” he said in a statement. “Red Ventures shares our vision and is committed to realizing the full potential of our portfolio of world-class brands.”
   Today we announced that Red Ventures has signed an agreement to acquire CNET Media Group.  For many years we’ve received interest from others looking to acquire the CMG portfolio, as it is one of the few long-term success stories in a very crowded and competitive digital media landscape.  That interest and this deal is a testament to what Mark Larkin and this team has built, especially in tandem with the overall growth and success of CBS Interactive and now the combined Viacom CBS Digital.  At our All Hands in August, I shared with you that CNET has continued to see record traffic numbers (even as it turned 25 this year!) and has built an incredible new commerce business across numerous properties. They’ve done all of this in one of the most challenging environments. I’m so proud of everything we have accomplished together.
 As Bob has said previously, Viacom CBS’s strategic priorities are focused on our networks business, studio production and, of course, our acceleration in streaming.  With these key areas of focus in mind, it was the right time to consider the interest of a partner who is committed to realizing the next chapter of growth for CMG. We believe we’ve found that right partner in Red Ventures. Today is the announcement of the purchase agreement and really just the beginning of the sale process. The CMG team will continue to be a part of Viacom CBS Digital until the deal closes, and we will continue to provide many central services to CMG for the coming months post-close as well. That said, I know it’s hard to think about saying goodbye to team members we’ve worked with for so long.  I want to thank the CMG team for the hard and smart work they’ve done to build and grow an incredible brand portfolio all the while taking risks, holding themselves to the highest journalism standards and contributing to our overall division and company goals for many years.  We wish them all the best on their next journey ahead and look forward to continuing to follow all their exploits and coverage in the future.
title: Red Ventures以5亿美元从ViacomCBS手中收购CNET Media Group
title_en: Red Ventures acquires CNET Media Group from ViacomCBS for $500M

note:
Thrive Capital领导了这轮融资，现有投资者Benchmark、Coatue、Caffeated Capital和CRV以及新投资者D1 Capital提供了额外资金。Airtable表示，目前已有20万家公司使用其服务，通过这项服务，Airtable总共筹集了约3.5亿美元。目前的客户包括Netflix、HBO、康泰纳仕娱乐公司(CondéNast Entertainment)、时代公司(Time)、洛杉矶市、麻省理工学院媒体实验室(MIT Media Lab)和IBM。
此外，该公司今天还推出了其最大的功能更新之一，开始在公司的整体平台愿景上执行，超越了目前的无代码能力，并为服务带来了更多的低码功能，以及新的自动化(想想Airtable的IFTTT)和数据管理工具。
正如Airtable创始人兼首席执行官刘豪伊告诉我的那样，自2018年该公司筹集C系列融资以来，许多投资者与该公司接洽，部分原因是市场明显意识到了低码/无码市场的潜在规模。
“我认为越来越多的市场认识到空间是真实存在的，而且空间非常大。”(…)。“他告诉我。“虽然我们并不严格需要资金，但它让我们能够继续积极投资，推进我们的平台、愿景，并真正积极执行，[…]。不用担心，‘嗯，COVID会发生什么？’有很多不确定性，对吗？我认为，即使是今天，明年的情况仍存在很大的不确定性。“。
在加州第一批避难所下单几个月后，该公司开始启动这轮融资，对于大多数投资者来说，这是一个纯粹的数字过程。
刘一直坦诚地表示，他希望长期建立这家公司-特别是在他早期将最后一家公司出售给Salesforce之后。作为一名创始人，这可能意味着他正试图保持在公司的高持股，尽管Airtable仍在继续筹集更多资金。不过，他认为，与法律和结构控制相比，与投资者保持一致才是最重要的。
他说：“我认为，实际上，在我看来，更重要的是与投资者达成哲学上的一致和预期上的一致。”“因为我不想陷入涉及公司未来的法律权利或结构性辩论的境地。在我看来，这几乎是最后的手段，因为它已经发展到了一个事情很丑陋的地方。我更愿意让坐在谈判桌上的所有投资者，无论他们是否拥有法律发言权，都完全与我们试图做的这项业务保持一致。“。
不过，与新资金同样重要的是该公司今天推出的各种新功能。也许其中最重要的是Airtable Apps。以前，Airtable用户可以使用预建的块将地图、甘特图和其他功能添加到他们的表格中。但是，虽然作为一项无代码的服务肯定有助于Airtable的用户开始使用，但总会不可避免地出现这样的情况，即预先构建的功能是不够的，用户需要更多的定制工具(刘将其称为排气阀)。因此，有了Airtable Apps，更老练的用户现在可以用JavaScript构建额外的功能-如果他们选择这样做，他们就可以在新的Airtable Marketplace中与其他用户共享这些新功能。
他指出：“你可能需要也可能不需要安全阀，显然，我们已经有20万个组织在使用没有这种安全阀的Airtable。”“但我认为，当你可以说，Airtable本身就有99%的份额，但最后1%是成败在此一举的时候，我们就会打开更多的用例。你需要它。然后，只要有了这个出口，并使其更具影响力，以1%的努力在Airtable上构建用例，而不是将整个堆栈应用程序构建为定制的应用程序，这就是所有的不同之处。“。
另一个主要的新功能是Airtable Automations。使用此功能，您可以构建自定义的自动化工作流来生成报告或执行其他重复步骤。您也可以通过服务的图形界面或使用JavaScript构建您自己的定制流和集成。目前，该功能是免费提供的，但团队正在研究如何随着时间的推移对其收费，因为如果您经常运行这些自动流，成本可能会变得很高。
最后一个新功能是Airtable Sync。有了这一点，团队可以更轻松地在组织内共享数据，同时还提供了对谁可以查看什么内容的控制。刘解释说：“我们的目标是让使用Airtable构建软件的人能够使该软件互联，并能够在我们表格的不同实例之间共享真值表的来源。”
note_en:
Thrive Capital   led the round, with additional funding by existing investors Benchmark, Coatue, Caffeinated Capital and CRV, as well as new investor D1 Capital. With this, Airtable, which says it now has 200,000 companies using its service, has raised a total of about $350 million. Current customers include Netflix, HBO, Condé Nast Entertainment, TIME, City of Los Angeles, MIT Media Lab and IBM.
 In addition, the company is also launching one of its largest feature updates today, which start to execute on the company’s overall platform vision that goes beyond its current no-code capabilities and bring more low-code features, as well new automation (think IFTTT for  Airtable)   and data management tools to the service.
  As Airtable founder and CEO  Howie Liu   told me, a number of investors approached the company since it raised its  Series C round in 2018, in part because the market clearly realized the potential size of the low-code/no-code market.
 “I think there’s this increasing market recognition that the space is real, and the spaces is very large […],” he told me. “While we didn’t strictly need the funding, it allowed us to continue to invest aggressively into furthering our platform, vision and really executing aggressively, […] without having to worry about, ‘well, what happens with COVID?’ There’s a lot of uncertainty, right? And I think even today there’s still a lot of uncertainty about what the next year will bear.”
  The company started opening the round a couple of months after the first shelter in place orders in California and for most investors, this was a purely digital process.
 Liu has always been open about the fact that he wants to build this company for the long haul — especially after he sold his last company to  Salesforce   at an early stage. As a founder, that likely means he is trying to keep his stake in the company high, even as Airtable continues to raise more money. He argues, though, that more so than the legal and structural controls, being aligned with his investors is what matters most.
 “I think actually, what’s more important in my view, is having philosophical alignment and expectations alignment with the investors,” he said. “Because I don’t want to be in a position where it comes down to a legal right or structural debate over the future of the company. That almost feels to me like the last resort where it’s already gotten to a place where things are ugly. I’d much rather be in a position where all the investors around the table, whether they have legal say or not, are fully aligned with what we’re trying to do with this business.”
 Just as important as the new funding though, are the various new features the company is launching today. Maybe the most important of these is Airtable Apps. Previously, Airtable users could use pre-built blocks to add maps, Gantt charts and other features to their tables. But while being a no-code service surely helped Airtable’s users get started, there’s always an inevitable point where the pre-built functionality just isn’t enough and users need more custom tools (Liu calls this an escape valve). So with Airtable Apps, more sophisticated users can now build additional functionality in JavaScript — and if they choose to do so, they can then share those new capabilities with other users in the new Airtable Marketplace.
  “You may or may not need an escape valve and obviously, we’ve gotten this far with 200,000 organizations using Airtable without that kind of escape valve,” he noted. “But I think that we open up a lot more use cases when you can say, well, Airtable by itself is 99% there, but that last 1% is make or break. You need it. And then, just having that outlet and making it much more leveraged to build that use case on Airtable with 1% effort, rather than building the full-stack application as a custom built application is all the difference.”
  The other major new feature is Airtable Automations. With this, you can build custom, automated workflows to generate reports or perform other repetitive steps. You can do a lot of that through the service’s graphical interface or use JavaScript to build you own custom flows and integrations, too. For now, this feature is available for free, but the team is looking into how to charge for it over time, given that these automated flows may become costly if you run them often.
 The last new feature is Airtable Sync. With this, teams can more easily share data across an organization, while also providing controls for who can see what. “The goal is to enable people who built software with Airtable to make that software interconnected and to be able to share a source of truth table between different instances of our tables,” Liu explained.
title: Airtable筹集了1.85亿美元，并推出了新的低码和自动化功能
title_en: Airtable raises $185M and launches new low-code and automation features

note_en:
Indulging in American food companies’ favorite pastime of marketing innovations that no one needs but potentially everyone wants,  Beyond Meat is launching Beyond Meatballs in grocery stores nationwide this week.
  The company’s meatballs have 30% less saturated fat and sodium than real meat and will be available at Whole Foods, Stop & Shop, Sprouts, Harris Teeter,  Kroger   and Albertsons, and more by early October, according to the statement.
  The suggested retail price for these pre-spiced and pre-rolled protein replacement balls of pea protein and other things* is $6.99 for 12 meatballs.
 For  Beyond Meat,   which already has a line of breakfast sausages and pre-made burgers under the “Cookout Classic” brand, the new product is the latest effort to win more of the meat aisle at the 26,000 outlets across the U.S. that stock the company’s products.
 “We’re thrilled to introduce Beyond Meatballs as they deliver on consumers’ growing demand for delicious and nutritious plant-based meat options without GMOs or synthetic ingredients,” said Stuart Kronauge, Chief Marketing Officer, Beyond Meat. “We are proud to introduce our newest innovation at retailers nationwide and know our fans will be excited about the great taste and convenience of Beyond Meatballs.”
 As part of the marketing campaign the company is offering free meatballs and spaghetti or a meatball hero at Beyond Meatball pop-up shops in New York and Los Angeles on Wednesday.
 Would-be Beyond Meatball eaters will have to reserve their complimentary meal and pick-up time in advance via The Beyond Meatball Shop’s LA and NY pages on Resy, while supplies last.
 *An earlier version of this article said that the meatballs would contain soy. They will not.
title: Beyond Meat正在全美各地的商店推出预先包装的肉丸
title_en: Beyond Meat is introducing pre-packaged meatballs at stores across America

该公司今天表示，它已经在捷克共和国、丹麦、德国、爱尔兰、意大利和拉脱维亚的官方应用程序的后端服务器和新建立的网关服务器之间启动了测试运行，网关服务器正在由T-Systems和SAP开发和建立，将在欧盟委员会位于卢森堡的数据中心进行操作。
这项服务将于10月份投入使用，这意味着拥有兼容应用的欧盟成员国将能够延长对在上榜国家集团内旅行的应用用户的数字联系人追踪。
欧盟委员会表示，网关服务将只交换最低限度的数据-即跟踪应用程序生成的任意标识符。
“交换的信息被假名、加密、保持在最低限度，并且只存储到追查感染所需的时间。它不允许个人身份识别，“它补充说。
现阶段只有分散的国家冠状病毒接触者追踪应用程序与网关服务兼容。虽然欧盟委员会表示，它将继续支持一些成员国正在开展的工作，以寻找方法将互操作性扩展到跟踪不同架构的应用程序，但目前尚不清楚这是否可行，而不会对隐私构成风险。
针对各国冠状病毒接触者追踪应用的互操作性计划的主要优势是，欧盟公民无需安装多个追踪应用-前提是他们前往该地区另一个国家旅行，该国家拥有兼容架构的国家应用。
然而，除了各种不同的应用程序架构选择外，一些欧盟成员国甚至还没有全国性的应用程序。因此，很明显，在可预见的未来，跨境覆盖范围将继续存在差距，这将增加打破(非国内)与旅行相关的冠状病毒传播列车的挑战。
note_en:
It’s kicked off test runs between the backend servers of the official apps from the Czech Republic, Denmark, Germany, Ireland, Italy and Latvia, and the newly established gateway server — which is being developed and set up by T-Systems and SAP, and will be operated from the Commission’s data centre in Luxembourg, it  said today.
 The service is due to become operational in October, meaning EU Member States with compatible apps will be able extend digital contacts tracing for app users travelling within the group of listed countries.
   The Commission says the gateway service will only exchange a minimum of data — namely the arbitrary identifiers generated by the tracing apps.
 “The information exchanged is pseudonymised, encrypted, kept to the minimum, and only stored as long as necessary to trace back infections. It does not allow the identification of individual persons,” it adds.
 Only decentralized national coronavirus contacts tracing apps are compatible with the gateway service at this stage. And while the Commission says it is continuing to support work being undertaken within some Member States to find ways to extend interoperability to tracing apps with different architectures, it’s not clear how viable that will be without risks to privacy.
 The main advantage of the interoperability plan for national coronavirus contacts tracing apps is to avoid the need for EU citizens to install multiple tracing apps — provided they’re traveling to another country in the region that has a national app with compatible architecture.
 However, in addition to varying choices of app architecture, some EU Member States don’t even have a national app yet. So it’s clear there will continue to be gaps in cross-border coverage for the foreseeable future which increases the challenge of breaking (non-domestic-)travel-related coronavirus transmission trains.
title: 欧洲开始测试APP互操作性服务，为跨境新冠肺炎暴露警报提供动力
title_en: Europe starts testing app interoperability service to power cross-border COVID-19 exposure alerts

9月14日至15日的IBMZ日将为数千名学生开启一场令人惊叹的企业计算之旅，他们将参加24小时的代码马拉松比赛。
我知道这是个大胆的说法。但我有16年的历史做后盾。这就是大型机大师大赛存在的时间，让学生们接触到运行世界大部分地区的鲜为人知但绝对必要的技术。
每年，成千上万的学生了解到大型机无处不在，可靠地运行着对我们现代生活至关重要的应用程序。每年，这些最优秀的学生都会完成这场比赛的所有三个级别，获得令人垂涎的第三级徽章。雇主知道，3级毕业生在市场上非常受欢迎，他们所做的不仅仅是学习运营他们业务的技术；这些学生拥有坚持不懈并独立解决复杂问题的能力。竞赛的第1级和第2级包括清晰的分步说明，而第3级的挑战要求学生找出如何使用公共文档解决问题。是的，这是一项基于真实世界…的竞赛挑战。需要解决问题和批判性思维能力！
参与者的回报也是丰厚的。每完成一级的学生，IBM将向联合国世界粮食计划署#ShareTheMeal捐款。随机抽签将给2级完成者一个带走500美元现金的机会。3级的全球获奖者将由一个受人尊敬的评委小组选出，他们将赢得2021年IBM重大活动的旅行津贴(如果不可能旅行，也可以赢得等值的现金)。最有价值的回报是你可以与高薪、有意义的技术工作建立联系。根据弗雷斯特研究公司(Forrester Research)最近的一项研究，大型机技术领域的工作比其他IT工作薪酬更高、更有意义，并提供更好的工作与生活平衡。
对于2020年，这是世界发生巨大变化的一年，我们决定把事情打扮得漂漂亮亮一点。新的竞赛管理平台带来了激动人心的新技术挑战，教您COBOL、Rexx和JCL等传统技术是如何与VS Code、Ansible和开放源码等新技术快速成为朋友的。
学生们将学习如何通过熟悉的工具使用世界上最强大的服务器。雇主已经告诉我们，他们已经准备好面试2020名获得徽章的人。为什么？3级完成者将通过此次竞赛展示他们已经知道如何实现大型机环境的现代化。即使是大型机专业人士也可以从完成这些挑战中受益，因为其中很多内容都是前沿的。
这么大的一场比赛只是在乞求一场盛大的活动来启动它，而IBM Z Day正好提供了所需的轰动效应。而“巨型”甚至还不足以描述我们在24小时内塞进的所有激动人心的事情：
开幕式将让参与者概述今年的比赛，有机会与2019年全球获奖者见面，并让年轻的企业计算专业人士有机会体验大型机生涯中的一天。
然后，作为一名IBM Z青年大使，在经验丰富的大型机专业人员和赞助Tech for Good组织的指导下，跟随IBM Z青年大使参加10场现场编码会议中的一场，通过级别1。
期待收到code.org、CSforAll和许多其他激动人心的非营利组织的来信，这些组织正在努力让年轻人为未来的工作做好准备。
结束语将把你介绍给那些正在积极寻找具备这些技能的求职者的雇主。学员还将通过学徒计划和IBM Z Bees了解其他职业道路。我们将以联合国世界粮食计划署的一句话作为结束，并反思学生们如何利用和引领科技向善，让世界变得更美好。
每完成一级的学生，我们将加倍向#ShareTheMeal捐款，前100名完成者将获得开放主机峰会的免费门票。
我们甚至会向出席人数最多的赞助组织奖励5000美元。
所以，请加入我们-参加一次会议，并帮助一家科技向善的非营利组织从IBM获得5000美元的资助！完成第一级，喂养四个孩子一天，并有所作为。传播消息：告诉您认识的学生加入我们，并帮助我们使这次在IBM Z日期间发布的Master the Mainframe成为一次值得纪念的大爆炸！
note_en:
IBM Z Day on September 14 – 15 will launch an amazing journey into enterprise computing for thousands of students with a 24-hour code-a-thon.
 That’s a bold statement, I know. But I have 16 years of history to back me up. That’s how long the  Master the Mainframe contest has existed, exposing students to the little-seen yet absolutely essential technology that runs much of this world.
 Every year, thousands of students learn that mainframes are everywhere, reliably running applications that are critical to our modern lives. And every year, the best of those students finish all three levels of this contest, earning the coveted Level 3 Badge. Employers know that Level 3 finishers are highly sought after in the marketplace and have done more than learn about the technologies running their businesses; these students have what it takes to persevere and solve complex problems independently. Levels 1 and 2 of the contest include clear step-by-step instructions, whereas Level 3’s challenges require students to figure out how to solve the problem using public documentation. Yes, this is a contest challenge based on the real world … requiring problem solving and critical thinking skills!
 The rewards for participants are substantial, as well. For every student who finishes Level 1, IBM will donate to the UN World Food Programme #ShareTheMeal. A random drawing will give Level 2 finishers a chance to walk away with $500 cash. The worldwide winners of Level 3, who will be chosen by a panel of esteemed judges, will win a travel stipend to a major IBM event in 2021 (or cash equivalent if travel isn’t possible). The most valuable reward is the connections you can make to high-paying, meaningful technology jobs. According to a  recent study by Forrester Research, jobs in mainframe technologies pay more, are more meaningful, and offer better work-life balance than other IT jobs.
 For 2020, a year of huge changes in the world, we decided to spruce things up a bit. So say hello to a new contest management platform with exciting new technical challenges that teach how legacy technologies like COBOL, REXX, and JCL are fast friends with new technologies like VS Code, Ansible, and open source.
 Students will learn how to use the world’s most powerful server with the familiar tools. Employers have already told us they are ready to interview 2020 badge earners. Why? Level 3 finishers will have demonstrated via this contest that they already know how to modernize mainframe environments. Even mainframe professionals can benefit from completing these challenges because so much of the content is cutting edge.
 A contest this big was just begging for a huge event to launch it, and IBM Z Day provides just the bang needed. And “huge” is not even big enough to describe all the exciting things we have crammed into 24 hours:
 Opening sessions will give participants an overview of this year’s contest, a chance to meet the 2019 worldwide winners, and an opportunity for young enterprise computing professionals to experience a day in the life of a mainframer.
 Then, follow along with one of 10 live coding sessions as an IBM Z Youth Ambassador goes through Level 1 under the guidance of an experienced mainframe professional and a sponsoring Tech for Good organization.
 Expect to hear from code.org, CSforAll, and many other exciting nonprofits that are working hard to empower young people to prepare for the future of work.
 Closing sessions will introduce you to employers who are actively searching for candidates with these skills. Participants will also learn about alternative career paths with the apprenticeship program and IBM Z Bees. We’ll close with a word from the UN World Food Programme and a reflection on how students can both use and lead Tech for Good and make the world a better place.
 For every student who finishes Level 1, we will  double the donation to #sharethemeal and the first 100 finishers will receive free tickets to the Open Mainframe Summit.
 We’ll even be awarding a $5,000 grant to the sponsor organization with the most attendees for their session.
 So please join us — attend a session and help a Tech for Good nonprofit win a $5,000 grant from IBM! Complete Level 1, feed four children for a day, and make a difference. Spread the word: Tell the students you know to join us and help us make this global launch of Master the Mainframe during IBM Z Day a Big Bang to remember!
title: IBM将为每个掌握大型机的学生提供一天四个孩子的食物
title_en: IBM Will Feed Four Children For a Day For Every Student Who Masters the Mainframe

note:
萨姆·班克曼-弗里德(Sam Bankman-Fry)带头将近10亿美元的投资者资金不流血地转移到陷入困境的交易所，赢得了喝彩。这是他的故事。
萨姆·班克曼-弗里德在担任SushiSwp的临时厨师时，成为了Defi&#39；的最新英雄。
周三，他监督了近10亿美元资金从Uniswap成功迁移到新的新兴交易所，并将其控制权交还给社区。
SushiSwp的长期未来仍然不确定，这一事件可能标志着Defi未来的方向发生了转变。
他们称其为“吸血鬼采矿”，即抽走交易所的资金，试图将其流动性抽走到另一个来源。在一场即使以加密货币标准衡量也一直处于紧张状态的戏剧中，分散的加密货币交易所Uniswap本周成为了受害者。克隆的交易所见证了价值近10亿美元的资金转移到其新的竞争对手SushiSwp，一个由社区领导的交易所的新纪元诞生了。
这个危险的企业-可能是分散金融领域的第一次敌意收购-几乎没有发生，最令人惊讶的是，它有一个幸福的结局。过去一周发生的事件也特别让一个人成为了聚光灯下的焦点。
萨姆·班克曼-弗里德(Sam Bankman-Fry)，28岁，是量化交易初创公司Alameda Research和集中式衍生品交易平台FTX的负责人。他已经成为SushiSwp的救世主；拯救了世界的超级明星英雄，安全地将用户的资金从Uniswap转移到新的交易所，然后将项目的控制权交还给其社区。
通过这样做，他可能已经永远不可挽回地改变了价值80亿美元的债务行业的方向。
在他在香港的办公室(在那里，他经常睡在豆袋上是出了名的)的一个电话中，他告诉DECRYPT为什么SushiSwp传奇远未结束。
Bankman-Fry说，SushiSwp项目是由一个匿名团队在两周前创建的，是“Uniswap的演变”。他们借鉴或复制了Uniswap的代码，同时增加了“面向社区的功能”，其中最引人注目的是寿司，以奖励流动性提供者(LP)，并将他们从Uniswap中吸引出来。
SushiSwp鼓励有限合伙人将代表他们在DEX的存款的Uniswap代币存入，以换取寿司。当时的计划是用Uniswap LP令牌交换标的资产，这样Uniswap的流动性就会变成SushiSwp的流动性。
它奏效了：在SushiSwp迁移之前的几天里，超过一半的Uniswap LP令牌被存入SushiSwp的迁移前计划。
但在这家暴发户通过耗尽Uniswap的资金来引导自己流动性的邪恶计划完成之前，密码市场就暴跌了。
SushiSwp的策划者Nomi厨师失去了勇气，最初兑现了他的代币-价值约1400万美元。他的行为被许多人解读为退出骗局。这一举动让这个羽翼未丰的社区措手不及，让这个项目陷入混乱，寿司的价格也在地板上。
在他套现引起的愤怒中，Nomi厨师随后做出了一个出人意料的举动：他将该项目的控制权移交给了他的主要批评者班克曼-弗里德(Bankman-Fry)。
“我他妈的晕倒了。我是说，我在豆袋上睡着了。有人大声说事情发生了。这完全是超现实的，“班克曼·弗里德说。
通过在FTX上上市，他参与了SushiSwp，通过执行一些聪明的交易，吸引了社区的钦佩，并在Twitter上提出了一些关于该项目的要点。
这通常不是运营项目的理由，“他挖苦地说。“它通常不会来自Twitter。”
但班克曼-弗里德专心于这项艰巨的任务-因为没有其他人有资格做这项工作；
他说：“如果没有人采取任何措施，事情很快就会变得明朗起来，寿司就要死了。”这个项目需要一个值得信赖的人，具有通过迁移的技术经验；一个了解SushiSwp及其背景的人，以及一个愿意接受挑战的人。
“这并不能描述那么多人。还有一件事让我臭名昭著，那就是承担一个新的、有点规模的、随机的项目。所以我认为，考虑到这一切，回过头来看，我似乎是理所当然地把它交给别人的人。“他说。
班克曼-弗里德并不是权力下放的坚定倡导者。他的职业生涯始于传统金融。他的父母是斯坦福大学(Stanford University)的法学教授；他在旧金山湾区出生长大，冒险前往东部的麻省理工学院(Massachusetts Institute Of Technology)学习物理，但很快就得出结论，学术界不适合他。取而代之的是，他加入了一家名为简街资本(Jane Street Capital)的量化交易公司，在那里他第一次遇到了加密货币。
他坚称，正是他对“有效利他主义”的渴望--积累财富以对世界产生积极影响--让他转行从事金融职业。他说，作为一名充满激情的素食者，班克曼-弗里德想要创造出能够提供帮助的财富，并建立一种使他能够真正有所作为的职业生涯。
考虑到这一点，2017年，他离开公司，用自己从家人和朋友那里借来的钱和资金创办了Alameda，并招募了麻省理工学院的前同学和华尔街的同龄人。Alameda是一家量化密码交易公司和流动性提供商，使用量化分析管理超过1亿美元的数字资产，并在每个主要市场和交易所运营。
这家创业公司是以加州的一个县命名的，当时班克曼-弗里德就住在那里。但是，在成立后不久，他将阿拉米达的办公室搬到了香港，以便更接近亚洲的情况。
对于阿拉米达的创始人来说，早期是一个陡峭的学习曲线。但他说，密码交易本身很容易，与他需要学习处理电汇、银行监管、合规、交易所限制以及困扰密码交易的其他微小操作困难的监管要求相比，密码交易本身很容易。
他说：“在你开始询问比特币是什么之前，就有大量的东西用于进行有利可图的套利或密码量化交易。”
但据彭博社(Bloomberg)报道，这是一个神奇的时刻，Alameda迅速确立了自己与普通的匿名密码交易业务的不同，这给了这家初创公司一个热情洋溢的评价。Alameda专注于套利和其他策略，据其网站显示，该公司拥有20多名员工，每天处理价值约10亿美元的交易。
在这一成功的背后，Bankman-Fry在密码和金融领域都建立了健康的客户和人脉网络，并于2019年春季推出了FTX，他的企业已经成为新兴Defi版图中的核心齿轮。
Bankman-Fry发起了另一次吸血鬼挖矿攻击，将用户的资金从Uniswap迁移到Sushiswap。
它花了六个小时，于周三完成，Bankman-Fry在整个行动过程中都在发推文。他被誉为英雄。但他是一个如此多产的推特用户，以至于他也被指责有一个文案团队与Twitter捆绑在一起。
他工作的最后一部分是将SushiSwp的控制权移交给社区，并邀请他们选择9个备受尊敬的“多签名”用户，这些用户可以作为一个小组(包括他自己)批准更改和支出。
(DECRYPT的马修·亚伦在周四主持班克曼-弗里德时获得了所有细节。)。
新的DEX现在已经上线并相当活跃。让Bankman-Fry感到兴奋的是，0xMaki(也被称为Maki)，这位伪匿名全栈工程师与首席Nomi一起帮助启动了SushiSwp，他提出要让这个项目重新站稳脚跟。
这个现已经过审计的项目的计划包括更流畅的用户界面，与其他Defi协议(如RenBTC)的集成，以及将其与UniSwp区分开来的其他功能。它未来甚至可以在以太以外的区块链上推出。
班克曼-弗里德说：“现在的问题是，它是否继续增长，是否缩小，是否演变成更大的东西，或者是否专门从事它现在正在做的事情。”他承认，他也担心创始人的行为对年轻的DEX造成的声誉损害将是持久的。
他说：“鉴于其奇怪的叙述，即它几乎一路坠落，然后又有所回升，目前还不完全清楚哪条弧线会在围绕它的公众叙述中胜出。”
还有一些阴谋论者认为诺米主厨就是班克曼-弗里德(Bankman-Fred)。他也否认了这一指控。
“我不知道诺米是谁，”他说。这不是我，我最好的猜测是，这很可能不是传言中的任何一个人。
在我们接受采访后不久，也就是周五，苏希斯瓦普的传奇故事又出现了意想不到的转折，诺米大厨忏悔并归还了他潜逃的1400万美元。
他表示有兴趣成为SushiSwp未来的一部分，尽管没有治理控制。
我真的很高兴诺米退还了资金，班克曼-弗里德在随后解密的消息中说。“我们不会忘记他的所作所为，但我们也不会忘记，最终他做出了正确的选择。”
总而言之，SushiSwap引起了相当大的争议，涉及克隆项目、集中控制与分散控制，以及产量农业提供的相互竞争的激励措施利用资本赚取资金回报的艺术，这也是Defi变得如此受欢迎的主要原因之一。
与一群未经考验的匿名开发人员相比，SushiSwp需要一双安全的手，拥有传统金融和集中交易的经验-这些正是DEX寻求取代的实体-才能实现其目标。这将是一个持久的遗产，并使该项目独一无二。
如果SushiSwp取得成功，甚至可能令人信服这样一种观点，即至少在一段时间内，集中控制的措施值得成功开发和推出创新的新Defi平台。
信和环球资本首席执行官马修·格雷厄姆(Matthew Graham)在接受DECRYPT采访时表示：“SushiSwp是一项令人着迷的经济实验。”他非常了解班克曼-弗里德银行，并已同意成为SushiSwp的多重签名持有者之一。
“考虑到Crypto有这么多不同的部落，很难想象还有谁会拥有必要的关系、软实力和技术印章来实现苏希斯瓦普的安全着陆。”在移民和多元化之后，现在轮到马基和寿司社区了，他们得到了第二次机会。我们迫不及待地想看看会发生什么。“他说。
班克曼-弗里德可能是DEFI世界的新手，但他对此并不陌生，FTX列出了大约35个DEFI令牌，他对这个空间非常了解。
他还对更深入的项目Serum感兴趣，这是FTX和Solana之间备受期待的非托管DEX合作，Solana是一个区块链平台，其特点是交易费用低，处理速度超快，并可与包括Etherum在内的其他平台互操作。
这使得Serve比其他DEX(如Balancer和Uniswap)具有明显的优势，并将Solana定位为Etherum的潜在挑战者，成为Defi的区块链标准。
note_en:
Sam Bankman-Fried won plaudits for spearheading a bloodless migration of almost $1 billion in investors’ funds to the beleaguered exchange. Here’s his story.
   Sam Bankman-Fried became DeFi's newest hero when he took on the role of temporary chef for SushiSwap.
 On Wednesday, he supervised the successful migration of almost one $1 billion in funds from Uniswap to the new, upstart exchange, and returned its control to the community.
 SushiSwap's longterm future is still uncertain, and the saga could mark a shift in direction for the future of DeFi.
 They call it “vampire mining,” the draining of an exchange’s funds in an effort to siphon its liquidity to another source. In a drama that’s been edge-of-your-seat, even by cryptocurrency standards, decentralized cryptocurrency exchange   Uniswap  became a victim of it this week. The cloned exchange saw almost a billion dollars worth of funds   migrated to its new rival SushiSwap , and a new era, in community-led exchanges, was born.
 This hazardous enterprise—possibly the first hostile takeover in the decentralized finance space—almost didn’t happen, and most surprisingly it had a happy ending. The events of the past week have also catapulted one man, in particular, into the limelight.
 Sam Bankman-Fried, 28, is head of quantitative trading startup   Alameda Research  and centralized derivatives trading platform   FTX . He’s become the SushiSwap savior; the superstar hero who saved the day, safely shepherding the user’s funds from Uniswap to the new exchange, and then returning control of the project to its community.
 In doing so, he may have irredeemably changed the direction of the   $8 billion DeFi industry  forever.
  On a call from his office in Hong Kong (where, often, he famously sleeps on a beanbag), he told   Decrypt  why the SushiSwap saga is far from over.
    The SushiSwap project was created just two weeks ago, by an anonymous team, as an “evolution of Uniswap,” Bankman-Fried said. They forked, or copied Uniswap’s code, while adding “community-oriented features,” most notably a token, SUSHI, to reward liquidity providers (LPs,) and draw them away from Uniswap.
 SushiSwap encouraged the LPs to deposit their Uniswap tokens, which represent their deposits in the DEX, in exchange for SUSHI. The plan was to then swap the Uniswap LP tokens for the underlying asset so that Uniswap liquidity would become SushiSwap liquidity.
 It worked: In the days leading up to the SushiSwap migration, more than half of all Uniswap LP tokens were deposited into SushiSwap’s pre-migration program.
 But before the upstart’s fiendish plan to bootstrap its own liquidity by draining Uniswap’s was accomplished, the crypto market took a dive.
 SushiSwap’s mastermind, Chef Nomi, lost his nerve, and initially cashed out his tokens—worth some $14 million. His actions were   construed by many as an exit scam . The move blindsided the fledgling community, leaving the project in disarray, and SUSHI’s price on the floor.
   Amidst the furor caused by his cashing out, Chef Nomi then made a surprise move: he transferred control of the project to Bankman-Fried, who had been his arch critic.
 “I was fucking floored. I mean, I was asleep on the beanbag. And someone shouted out that it happened. And it was totally surreal,” said Bankman Fried.
 He’d gotten involved with SushiSwap through its listing on FTX, through executing some clever trades that drew the community’s admiration and making some salient points about the project on Twitter.
   "That’s not generally grounds to be running a project,” he said, wryly. “It doesn't usually come from Twitter.”
   But Bankman-Fried buckled down to the gargantuan task—because no one else was qualified to do the job;
 “It quickly became clear that SUSHI was dying, if no one did anything,” he said. The project needed someone who was trustworthy, with the technical experience to make it through migration; someone who knew about SushiSwap, and its context, and someone willing to take on a challenge.
 “That doesn't describe that many people. And one thing I'm notorious for is taking on a new, somewhat sizable, random project. And so I think given all that, looking back on it, it seems like I was the natural person to hand it off to,” he said.
   Bankman-Fried is no dyed-in-the-wool advocate of decentralization. He started his career in traditional finance. His parents were law professors at Stanford University; he was born and raised in the Bay Area and ventured east to study physics at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, but quickly came to the conclusion that academia was not for him. Instead, he joined a quantitative-trading firm, Jane Street Capital, where he first encountered cryptocurrencies.
  He insists it was his desire for “effective altruism”—accumulating wealth to make a positive impact in the world—that turned him onto finance as a career. A passionate vegan, Bankman-Fried wants to make wealth that works to help, and to build a career that will enable him to make a real difference, he said.
 With that in mind, in 2017, he left to start Alameda, with his own money and funds borrowed from family and friends and recruited former MIT classmates and Wall Street contemporaries. Alameda is a quantitative crypto trading firm and liquidity provider, that uses quantitative analysis to manage over $100 million in digital assets, and operates on every major market and exchange.
   The startup is named after the Californian county, where Bankman-Fried was living at the time. But, soon after launch, he moved Alameda’s offices to Hong Kong, to be closer to the action in Asia.
 The early days were a steep learning curve for Alameda’s founder. But crypto trading itself was easy he said, compared to regulatory requirements he needed to learn to handle wire transfers, banking regulations, compliance, exchange limits, and the other minute operational difficulties that beset crypto trades.
 “There's a ton of stuff that goes into doing profitable arbitrage or quant trading in crypto before you even get to asking what a Bitcoin is,” he said.
  But it was a magical moment when it all came together, and Alameda rapidly established itself as distinct from the average, anonymous, crypto-trading operation, according to Bloomberg, which   gave the startup a glowing write up . Alameda focuses on arbitrage and other strategies, has over 20 employees, and handles around a billion dollars worth of daily trades, according to its website.
 On the back of this success, having built a healthy network of clients and connections in both crypto and finance, Bankman-Fried launched FTX, in the spring of 2019, and his enterprises have become central cogs in the emerging DeFi landscape.
     Bankman-Fried   initiated yet another vampire mining attack  to migrate users’ funds from Uniswap to Sushiswap.
 It took six hours and was completed on Wednesday, with Bankman-Fried   tweeting  throughout the entire operation. He was hailed as a hero. But he’s such a prolific tweeter that he was also accused of having a team of copywriters chained to Twitter.
 The last part of his job was turning control of SushiSwap over to the community, and inviting them to choose nine well-respected “multisig” users who could approve changes and expenditures as a group (including himself.)
   (  Decrypt ’s Matthew Aaron, scored all the details when he hosted Bankman-Fried on Thursday. )
  The new DEX is now live and reasonably active. And Bankman-Fried is excited that 0xMaki (also known as Maki), the pseudo-anonymous full-stack engineer who helped launch SushiSwap alongside Chief Nomi, has offered to get the project back on its feet.
   Plans for the now-audited project  include a smoother user interface, integration with other DeFi protocols, such as RenBTC, and other features to distinguish it from UniSwap. It could even launch on blockchains other than Ethereum in the future.
  “There’s a question of whether it keeps growing, whether it shrinks, whether it morphs into something even bigger, or whether it specializes in what it's doing now,” said Bankman-Fried. He admitted that he also worries that the reputational damage to the young DEX engendered by the actions of its founder will be lasting.
   “Given its weird narrative, that it crashed almost all the way down and then came back up somewhat, it's still not totally clear which arc is going to win out in the public narrative around it,” he said.
 Then there are the conspiracy theorists who believe that Chef Nomi   is  Bankman-Fried. He denies that allegation too.
 "I don’t know who Nomi is," he said. "It’s not me and my best guess is that it’s probably not any of the people it has been rumored to be."
   Not long after our interview, on Friday, the SushiSwap saga took another unexpected twist when Chef Nomi repented and returned the $14 million he had absconded with.
   He expressed interest in being part of SushiSwap’s future, albeit with no governance control.
 "I'm really glad that Nomi returned the funds," Bankman-Fried said, in a follow-up message to   Decrypt . “We won't forget what he did, but we also won't forget that in the end, he made the right choice.”
   In all, SushiSwap has generated   considerable controversy , about   cloned projects ,   centralized versus decentralized control , and the competing incentives offered by yield farming—the art of using capital to earn a return on funds, and one of the main reasons why DeFi has become so popular.
 In contrast to a bunch of untested, anonymous developers, a safe pair of hands, with experience of traditional finance and centralized exchanges—the very entities that DEXs seek to replace—were needed for SushiSwap to reach its goal. That will be a lasting legacy and makes the project unique.
 If SushiSwap is successful, it may even give credence to the argument that a measure of centralized control, at least for a time, is worth it to successfully develop and launch innovative new DeFi platforms.
 “SushiSwap is a fascinating economic experiment,” Matthew Graham, CEO of Sino Global Capital, told   Decrypt . He knows Bankman-Fried well and has agreed to be one of SushiSwap’s multisig holders.
 “Given that crypto has so many different tribes, it's hard to think of anyone else who would have had the requisite relationships, soft power, and tech chops to pull off SushiSwap's safe landing. Post-migration and multisig, it's now up to Maki and the Sushi community, having been given a second chance. We can't wait to see what happens,” he said.
 Bankman-Fried may be a relative newbie in the DeFi world, but he’s no stranger to it, with FTX listing some 35 DeFi tokens, he knows the space well.
 And he’s interested in going deeper, with   Project Serum , a highly anticipated, non-custodial, DEX collaboration between FTX and   Solana , a blockchain platform characterized by low transaction fees, an ultra-fast processing speed, and interoperability with other platforms, including Ethereum.
 That gives Serum a distinct advantage over other DEXs, like Balancer and Uniswap, and positions Solana as a potential challenger to Ethereum as the blockchain standard for DeFi.
title: 看看SushiSwp对Uniswap的快速、动荡和成功的“敌意收购”，Uniswap是一种用于在以太上自动提供流动性的去中心化协议
title_en: A look at SushiSwap's quick, turbulent, and successful “hostile takeover” of Uniswap, a decentralized protocol for automated liquidity provision on Ethereum

周一早些时候，该零售商宣布与医疗用品无人机送货公司Zipline进行试运行。只有沃尔玛的医疗保健和保健产品将是试点计划的一部分，而且它只会在2021年初出现在阿肯色州西北部。
无人机将覆盖位于阿肯色州本顿维尔的沃尔玛总部周围50英里的半径。它们可以运载多达4磅的货物，飞行速度高达每小时80英里。
在网上或通过沃尔玛应用程序订购的商品会被放入连接到无人机上的Zipline送货箱中。这些产品，无论是头痛药还是消毒湿巾，都来自附近的沃尔玛商店。然后，订单会按照预先设定的路线飞到顾客的门口，箱子就会在那里降落伞。无人机返回到枢纽或基地。
Zipline之前曾在卢旺达和加纳工作，用无人机运送医疗用品。自从新冠肺炎疫情爆发以来，它一直在向北卡罗来纳州的一家医院运送个人防护设备和药品。试用期结束后，沃尔玛表示，送货计划可能会扩大，无论是在覆盖范围还是无人机携带的内容方面。
沃尔玛正在与亚马逊(Amazon)竞争，亚马逊上个月获得了美国联邦航空局(FAA)对其Prime Air无人机配送系统的批准。这家在线零售商还没有开始，但它计划在30分钟内送货。
在北卡罗来纳州，沃尔玛已经在与一家以色列无人机公司测试无人机送货。这些飞过的订单本周开始减少。
note_en:
The retailer announced a trial run with medical supply drone delivery company  Zipline early Monday. Only medical and health and wellness products from Walmart will be part of the pilot program and it'll only be in Northwest Arkansas in early 2021.
 The drones will cover a 50-mile radius around Walmart headquarters in Bentonville, Arkansas. They can haul up to four pounds of cargo and fly up to 80 mph.
 Items ordered online or through the Walmart app are placed into a Zipline delivery box which attaches to the drone. The products, whether headache medicine or disinfecting wipes, come from a nearby Walmart store. The order then flies a preprogrammed route to the customer's door where the box parachutes down. The drone returns back to a hub or base.
  Zipline has previously worked in Rwanda and Ghana to delivery medical supplies by drone. Since the COVID-19 outbreak it's been delivering personal protective equipment and medicine to a North Carolina hospital. After the trial period Walmart said the delivery program could expand, both in coverage and what the drones carry.
  Walmart is racing against Amazon,  which received FAA approval for its Prime Air drone delivery system last month. The online retailer hasn't started yet, but it plans to deliver orders within 30 minutes.
 In North Carolina,  Walmart is already testing drone deliveries with an Israeli drone company. Those fly-by orders started dropping this week.
title: 沃尔玛宣布2021年初与Zipline合作试运行，通过无人机在阿肯色州西北部交付医疗、健康和健康产品
title_en: Walmart announces a trial run with Zipline to deliver medical, health, and wellness products by drone in Northwest Arkansas in early 2021

正在与数字营销公司达成5亿美元的交易，出售媒体集团(Media Group)。数字营销公司周一宣布了这笔交易。
根据协议，CNET媒体集团执行副总裁和总经理马克·拉金，以及该部门的高级管理人员，将在收购后继续留在Red Ventures。CNet的出售预计将在2020年第四季度完成。CNET传媒集团有980名员工，“我们的计划是以意想不到的方式发展CNET，”一位Red Ventures的代表告诉“综艺”杂志。她拒绝回答公司在交易完成后是否预计会有裁员，她说，“目前我们关注的是我们的增长方式。”
哥伦比亚广播公司(CBS)于2008年以18亿美元收购了CNET Networks。除了CNET的旗舰消费技术网站，CNET媒体集团还拥有包括GameSpot、Metacritic Review-Aggregation网站、TVGuide.com、Chowhound、Roadshow和B2B技术网站ZDNet在内的品牌。
拉金在一份声明中说：“我相信，Red Ventures的客户体验平台与CNET媒体集团丰富的内容和深厚的编辑专业知识相结合，对我们的观众和我们的合作伙伴都有极大的好处。”红色风险投资公司与我们有着相同的愿景，并致力于实现我们世界级品牌组合的全部潜力。“。
成立于2000年的Red Ventures表示，它在美国、英国和巴西拥有3000名员工。这家总部位于南卡罗来纳州米尔堡的公司拥有并运营数字品牌，包括Bankrate、The Points Guy、Healthline、MyMove和Allconnect.com。
Red Ventures的现有品牌在家庭服务、健康、金融、旅游、教育和娱乐等垂直领域开展业务。据该公司称，CNET媒体集团的加入“加速了”Red Ventures进入新的垂直领域，包括消费技术和游戏。
Red Ventures首席执行官兼联合创始人里克·埃利亚斯(Ric Elias)在宣布这笔交易时表示：“在过去的25年里，CNET Media Group建立了一个充满活力的品牌组合，在消费科技和游戏等主题上拥有应得的权威，这些主题在人们的生活中发挥着越来越重要的作用。”红色风险投资公司渴望以更个性化的消费体验投资于CNET媒体集团的发展，这将使CNET媒体集团的品牌重新焕发活力，并为所有人带来前所未有的机会。“。
CNET交易延续了Red Ventures在过去三年中扩大面向消费者的足迹的汇总和合作战略。今年早些时候，时代公司(Time Inc.)。宣布与Red Ventures合作推出一个新的个人理财网站，名为NextAdvisor。2019年，Red Ventures收购了Healthline Media，后者包括Healthline.com、Greatist.com和今日医疗新闻(MNT)。2017年，Red Ventures以12.4亿美元收购了Bankrate个人理财网站，这笔交易包括Points Guy和CreditCards.com。
自维亚康姆和哥伦比亚广播公司(CBS)去年完成合并以来，维亚康姆CBS首席执行官鲍勃·巴克什(Bob Bakish)曾表示，这家媒体集团正在评估出售非核心资产。今年早些时候，巴克什表示，该公司正在寻求出售图书出版商西蒙·舒斯特(Simon&amp；Schuster)，分析人士称，这笔交易的价格可能高达15亿美元。Bakish此前表示，ViacomCBS还在探索出售黑岩(Black Rock)，这座位于曼哈顿中城的写字楼自20世纪60年代中期以来一直是CBS的总部。
CNET于1994年首次推出，当时正值互联网繁荣的早期。该同名网站涵盖技术、科学和文化，包括新闻、评论、购买指南、特写和评论，并运营西班牙语版本CNET en Español。
对于CNet Media Group的出售，Evercore将担任Red Ventures的财务顾问，K&amp；L Gates LLP将担任Red Ventures的法律顾问。花旗(Citi)担任维亚康姆哥伦比亚广播公司(ViacomCBS)的财务顾问，Searman&amp；Sterling LLP担任法律顾问。
note_en:
is offloading   Media Group in a $500 million deal with digital-marketing company  , which announced the deal Monday.
 Under the deal, CNET Media Group EVP and GM Mark Larkin, along with the unit’s senior execs, will remain with Red Ventures following the acquisition. The CNET sale is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2020. CNET Media Group has 980 employees and “our plan is to grow CNET in ways unforeseen,” a Red Ventures rep told  Variety. She declined to address whether the company anticipates any layoffs following the deal close, saying that “right now we’re focusing on how we grow.”
 CBS  acquired CNET Networks in 2008 for $1.8 billion. In addition to the flagship CNET consumer-tech site, the CNET Media Group also houses brands including GameSpot, the Metacritic reviews-aggregation site, TVGuide.com, Chowhound, Roadshow, and B2B tech site ZDNet.
  “I believe that the combination of Red Ventures’ customer-experience platform and CNET Media Group’s rich content and deep editorial expertise greatly benefits both our audiences and our partners,” Larkin said in a statement. “Red Ventures shares our vision and is committed to realizing the full potential of our portfolio of world-class brands.”
 Founded in 2000, Red Ventures says it has 3,000 employees in the U.S., the U.K. and Brazil. The Fort Mill, S.C.-based company owns and operates digital brands including Bankrate, the Points Guy, Healthline, MyMove and Allconnect.com.
 Red Ventures’ existing brands operate in the home services, health, finance, travel, education and entertainment verticals. The addition of CNET Media Group “accelerates” Red Ventures’ entry into new verticals, including consumer technology and gaming, according to the company.
 “Over the last 25 years CNET Media Group has built a dynamic portfolio of brands with well-earned authority on such topics as consumer tech and gaming that play an increasingly important role in people’s lives,” Ric Elias, Red Ventures CEO and co-founder, said in announcing the deal. “Red Ventures is eager to invest in CNET Media Group’s growth with more personalized consumer experiences that will reinvigorate CNET Media Group’s brands and unlock unprecedented opportunity for all.”
 The CNET deal continues Red Ventures’ roll-up and partnership strategy to expand its consumer-facing footprint over the past three years. Earlier this year, Time Inc. announced a partnership with Red Ventures to launch a new personal-finance site called NextAdvisor. In 2019, Red Ventures acquired Healthline Media, which includes Healthline.com, Greatist.com and Medical News Today (MNT). In 2017, Red Ventures acquired the Bankrate personal-finance website for $1.24 billion, a deal that encompassed the Points Guy and CreditCards.com.
 Since the merger of Viacom and CBS was completed last year, ViacomCBS CEO Bob Bakish has said the media conglomerate was evaluating selling non-core assets. Earlier this year, Bakish said  the company was seeking to sell book publisher Simon & Schuster, which analysts say could fetch upwards of $1.5 billion. Bakish previously said  ViacomCBS also exploring a sale of Black Rock, the office building in midtown Manhattan which has served as CBS’s headquarters since the mid-1960s.
  CNET first launched in 1994, in the early days of the internet boom. The namesake site covers technology, science and culture with news, reviews, buying guides, features and commentary, and operates a Spanish-language version, CNET en Español.
 For the CNET Media Group sale, Evercore is serving as financial adviser and K&L Gates LLP is acting as legal adviser to Red Ventures. Citi is serving as financial adviser and Shearman & Sterling LLP is acting as legal adviser to ViacomCBS.
title: Red Ventures表示，已同意以5亿美元从ViacomCBS手中收购CNET Media Group，交易预计将于2020年第四季度完成
title_en: Red Ventures says it has agreed to buy CNET Media Group from ViacomCBS for $500M in a deal expected to close in Q4 2020

note:
Oculus Quest 2 VR头盔通过上传到母公司Facebook运营的营销中心的两个宣传视频全面泄露，视频概述了这款独立头盔的规格，这款头盔是2019年Oculus Quest的继任者。网站上还列出了一对耳机的现场演示，定于9月16日和17日进行。这与之前传言的9月15日“任务2”的发射日期不谋而合。
根据视频，这款新耳机由高通的骁龙XR2平台驱动，该平台是专门为AR和VR硬件设计的(与最初为Quest提供动力的通用骁龙835不同)。其中一段视频夸耀说，Quest 2拥有“几乎4K的显示屏”，翻译过来就是“每只眼睛将近2K”，另一段视频说，这比最初的Quest增加了50%的像素。这款耳机还具有6 GB的RAM(最初为4 GB)和最高256 GB的存储空间(最高为128 GB)，以及3D位置音频，并支持免控制器手部跟踪功能。
虽然这些视频提供了大量关于Oculus Quest 2的新细节，但仍有一些挥之不去的问题。首先，还不清楚显示器的刷新率会是多少，之前的报告指出，为了显示更流畅的运动，哪些频率可能会从72 Hz增加到90 Hz，甚至120 Hz。也没有提到耳机的大小或重量。此前有报道称，Quest 2的体积会比最初的Quest要小，但视频只是简单地将新硬件称为“流线型”和“轻量化”。
视频中确实提到了一种新的软触摸表带，他们说这使得耳机更容易滑上和滑下，以及一对更新的控制器，Oculus声称这种控制器具有“新的人体工程学”，应该会让它们使用起来更舒适。Quest 2还提供Oculus Link支持，允许您使用USB-C线将耳麦连接到PC，并使用它玩基于PC的VR游戏。
这不是我们第一次看到这款新耳机，它的图片早在7月份就泄露了。
Oculus Quest 2似乎将成为Facebook将发布的第一款耳机，此前Facebook做出了一项有争议的决定，要求Oculus用户必须拥有Facebook账户。尽管该公司现有耳机的用户在2023年之前可以使用单独的Oculus账户，但Facebook表示，未来的设备将需要Facebook登录，无论你是否已经有了单独的Oculus账户。
目前还不清楚这款耳机的价格，但沃尔玛现已删除的一份清单显示，其256 GB的机型零售价可能为399美元。Facebook Connect开发者大会定于9月16日开始，官方声明很可能不会太远。
更正：本文的一个较早版本称，最初的Oculus Quest有一个60 Hz的显示屏。这是不正确的，它实际上有一个72赫兹的显示器。之前的报告还表明，正如我们之前所说的那样，耳机将变得更小，而不是更大。
note_en:
The Oculus Quest 2 VR headset has leaked in full via a  pair of  promotional videos uploaded to a marketing hub run by parent company Facebook, The videos outline the specs of the standalone headset, which is a successor to 2019’s  Oculus Quest. Also listed  on the site is a pair of live demos of the headset, scheduled for September 16th and 17th. That lines up neatly with a  previously-rumored launch date for the Quest 2 of September 15th.
 According to the videos, the new headset is powered by Qualcomm’s Snapdragon XR2 platform, which is specifically designed for AR and VR hardware (unlike the general purpose Snapdragon 835 that powered the original Quest). One video boasts that the Quest 2 has an “almost 4K display,” which it says translates to “nearly 2K per eye,” which a  second video says is 50 percent more pixels than the original Quest. The headset also has 6GB of RAM (up from 4GB) and up to 256GB of storage (up from a maximum of 128GB), as well as 3D positional audio, and the  return of controller-free hand tracking.
  Although the videos provide plenty of new details about the Oculus Quest 2, there are a couple of lingering questions. It’s not clear, for a start, what the display’s refresh rate will be, which  previous reports indicated could increase from 72Hz to 90Hz or even 120Hz to display smoother motion. There’s also no mention of the headset’s size or weight. Previous reports suggested that the Quest 2 would be less bulky than the original Quest, but the videos simply refer to the new hardware as “streamlined” and “light.”
  The videos do mention a new soft-touch strap which they say make the headset easier to slip on and off as well as a pair of updated controllers with “new ergonomics” that Oculus claims should make them more comfortable to use. Oculus Link support also returns for the Quest 2, allowing you to use a USB-C cable to connect the headset to a PC and use it to play PC-based VR games.
 This isn’t our first look at the new headset, images of which leaked  back in  July.
  The Oculus Quest 2 looks set to be the first headset Facebook will release following its controversial decision to  require Oculus users to have a Facebook account. Although users of the company’s existing headsets can use a separate Oculus account until 2023, Facebook has said future devices will require a Facebook login, regardless of whether you already have a separate Oculus account.
 There’s no word on how much the headset is due to cost, but a now-removed  Walmart listing suggested its 256GB variant could retail for $399. With the Facebook Connect developer conference scheduled to start  on September 16th, it’s likely that an official announcement isn’t too far away.
 Correction:  An earlier version of this article said that the original Oculus Quest had a 60Hz display. This is incorrect, it actually had a 72Hz display. Previous reports also indicated that the headset would be getting less bulky, not more, as we previously stated.
title: 泄露的宣传视频显示，Oculus Quest 2由高通的SnapdraonXR2驱动，配备“几乎4K显示屏”、6 GB RAM、高达256 GB的存储空间和3D音频
title_en: Leaked promotional videos show Oculus Quest 2 powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon XR2 with an “almost 4K display”, 6GB RAM, up to 256GB storage, and 3D audio

note:
新冠肺炎的流行鼓励了包括Hulu、Plex和亚马逊视频在内的许多流媒体服务采用类似的协同观看功能，或者至少非正式地允许Netflix Party等支持协同观看的第三方应用程序。
然而，Facebook并不是简单地加入了这股潮流。实际上，它在两年前的F8开发者大会上宣布了为Messenger开发共同观看体验的计划。从那以后，该产品一直在开发中。
在Messenger中推出类似协同查看的功能是一项重大的技术挑战。该服务连接到Facebook Watch CDN(内容分发网络)，将视频放入Messenger。然后，它还开发了一种系统，允许同一视频的不同用户流之间进行紧密同步。这也很困难，因为农村地区的人可能比城市地铁里的人带宽速度慢，但这项服务必须确保他们同时看到相同的视频部分。
要使用该功能，用户必须首先进入Messenger视频通话或Messenger Room-它不能从Facebook Watch选项卡或其他任何地方启动。在Messenger中，他们可以通过从屏幕底部拉出的抽屉中选择新选项来开始共同观看会话。Facebook表示，最初，这一功能只在iOS和Android移动设备上可用。
从出现的界面中，用户可以浏览或搜索找到要一起观看的内容。Facebook Watch的内容分为“电视电影”、“已观看”(您最近观看的内容)、“已上传”(您自己的视频)和“建议”等类别。
虽然用户不能创建播放列表或队列，但所有参与者都可以帮助选择视频。也就是说，没有一个人为其他人指导体验。每个人都可以在视频中停止、暂停、向前或向后跳跃。
虽然许多人可能会将这一功能用于某种靠背的共同观看体验，但它还有其他潜力。例如，一群人可以聚在一起看健身视频，一起锻炼。
虽然官方音乐视频最近可以在Facebook Watch上观看，但由于Facebook与唱片公司达成了新的协议，这些视频不会立即出现在这种共同观看的体验中。然而，Facebook表示，他们将在未来几周内加入，首先是在美国、印度和泰国。每个市场还将在“电视和电影”部分展示本地化内容。
这一功能将开始在全球范围内通过iOS和Android推出，但Facebook打算在几周内准备好网络支持，其他平台，如台式机，也将紧随其后。
note_en:
The COVID-19 pandemic has encouraged a number of streaming services — including Hulu, Plex and Amazon Video — adopt similar co-viewing features, or at the very least, unofficially permit third-party apps that enable co-watching, like Netflix Party.
 However, Facebook didn’t just jump on this bandwagon. It actually announced its plans to develop a co-viewing experience for Messenger at its F8 developer conference two years ago. And the product has been in development ever since.
   To roll out something like co-viewing across Messenger is a significant technical challenge. The service hooks into the Facebook Watch CDN (content distribution network), to pull the videos into Messenger. It also then developed a system that allows for tight synchronization between the various users’ streams of the same video. That, too, can be difficult, as someone in a rural area may have slower bandwidth speeds than someone in an urban metro, but the service has to make sure they’re seeing the same part of the video at the same time.
  To use the feature, users have to first be in a Messenger video call or a Messenger Room — it can’t be kicked off from the Facebook Watch tab or anywhere else. From Messenger, they’re able to start a co-watching session by selecting the new option from a drawer that pulls up from the bottom of the screen. Initially, this feature is only available on iOS and Android mobile devices, Facebook says.
 From the interface that appears, users can then browse or search to find something to watch together. Facebook Watch content is organized into categories like “TV & Movies,” “Watched” (your recently watched content), “Uploaded” (your own videos), and “Suggested.”
 While users can’t create playlists or queues, all participants can help choose videos. That is, there’s not one person directing the experience for others. Everyone can also stop, pause, jump forward or back in the videos, too.
 Though many may use the feature for a sort of lean-back co-watching experience, it has other potential. For example, a group could get together to watch a fitness video and workout together.
 Though official music videos recently became available on Facebook Watch, thanks to Facebook’s new deal with record labels, they’re not immediately available in this co-viewing experience. However, Facebook says that they’ll be added in the next few weeks, initially in the U.S., India and Thailand to start. Each market will also feature localized content under the “TV & Movies” section, too.
 The feature will begin rolling out globally across iOS and Android, but Facebook intends to have web support ready in a matter of weeks, and other platforms, like desktop, will follow.
title: Facebook为Messenger推出了Watch Together，最多可以让8人移动聊天或50人在一个房间里一起观看视频
title_en: Facebook introduces Watch Together for Messenger, letting up to eight people in a mobile chat or 50 people in a Room watch videos together

子串移除游戏是Codeforce的一个800分的问题，我希望你已经仔细阅读了这个问题，并尝试过解决它。这个问题从根本上说，顾名思义，就是删除一个子字符串。
每次测试，您都会得到一个二进制字符串(0和1)，您需要删除每个连续的1才能使Alice获胜。爱丽丝是谁？爱丽丝和鲍勃是这个游戏中的玩家，谁去掉了最高数字1&39；谁就是赢家。你的职责是让爱丽丝获胜，因为爱丽丝是第一个移动的。
你可以把这个问题想成是先捕获字符串中所有连续的一个，然后我们才能决定我们应该专注于哪个1&#39；才能让Alice获胜。例如，如果输入是01111001，这意味着我们有4个连续的1；，然后只有1；要使爱丽丝在这种情况下获胜，我们应该让她走那一步，这显然是她已经走的第一步，然后鲍勃可以走第二步，也就是字符串中的最后一步。
让我们再举一个例子，111111这是6个，这可能是爱丽丝的第一步，所以6个步骤将是她的胜利。
所以一共有5个..。它们中的哪一个应该是爱丽丝的动作呢？那将是第一个，第三个和第五个。所以这里是爱丽丝的3步棋。
这里，我们有2个连续的，4个连续的，3个连续的。哪些招式可以让爱丽丝获胜？因此，我们需要考虑如何才能使移动次数尽可能高，因此，如果我们按降序对多个连续的移动进行排序，然后从最高的数字开始，这将使我们得到Alice的第一个移动，第二个移动应该是从该移动开始的第二步。因此，在本例中：[4，3，2]Alice将采取动作4和2，它们的总和为6。

note_en:
Substring Removal Game is an 800-point problem at codeforces, I hope you have read the problem carefully and have tried thinking about solving it. This problem basically states as its name sounds to say, to remove a substring.
 Per each test, you're given a string of binary characters (0's and 1's) and you're required to remove each consecutive 1's to make Alice win. Who is Alice? Alice and Bob are players in the game, and the winner is the one who removes the highest numbers of 1's. Your duty is to make Alice win given that Alice is the first to move.
    You can think of this problem as capturing all consecutive ones in the string first and then we can make a decision on which 1's we should focus on to make Alice win. For example, if the input is 01111001 this means that we have 4 consecutive 1's and then just 1; to make Alice win in this case, we should make her take that move which is obviously her first move already and then Bob can take the second move which is the last 1 in the string.
 Let's have another example, 111111 these are 6 ones which can be the first move for Alice so 6 moves will be her win.
  So there are 5 ones .. which of them should be Alice's moves? That would be the first and the third and the fifth .. so here 3 moves for Alice.
  Here, we have 2 consecutive ones, 4 consecutive ones, and 3 consecutive ones. Which moves can make Alice win? so we need then to think how we can make number of moves the highest possible so if we sort numbers of consecutive ones in descending order and then start on the highest number that would give us the first move for Alice, and the second move should be a step two from that one. So in this case: [4 3 2] Alice will take moves 4 and 2 which sum up to 6.
